title: Highest temperature in Seattle on March 23?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 23 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/wa/seatac/KSEA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' near-unanimous consensus on a Seattle high of 50-51°F on March 23 stems from National Weather Service forecasts pinpointing a cool, overcast day with marine layer persistence under a weak upper-level trough, yielding model consensus highs around 51°F from GFS and ECMWF ensembles. Historical March data from NOAA shows Seattle's average high near 52°F, with recent cool anomalies reinforcing this positioning amid Pacific Northwest's typical spring chill. Realistic challenges include an unforeseen ridge amplifying downslope warming or rapid cloud dissipation boosting solar insolation by 10-15°F, though low-probability given current 500mb patterns and climatological baselines.
